name: Umm Farwa
title: Mourning Mother
shortDescription: Wife of Imam Hasan (AS) and one of the mourning mothers in the Karbala caravan who endured captivity.
fullStory:
Umm Farwa (some consider her to be Ramla, while others consider her a different woman) was one of Imam Hasan Mujtaba's (AS) wives. After Imam Hasan's martyrdom, she lived with her children under the protection of Imam Hussain (AS) and traveled with the caravan from Medina to Karbala.

On Ashura, she was one of the women present in the tents, witnessing the painful martyrdom of the Bani Hashem youth. With the patience and steadfastness she learned from the Ahl al-Bayt's school, she bore the grief of her loved ones.

After the battle ended, when the tents were looted and set on fire, Umm Farwa, along with other women and children, was taken captive by the enemy and traveled the harsh and grueling route from Kufa to Sham in chains.

She is a symbol of mothers and women who, although they did not fight with swords in the battlefield, played an important role in keeping the message of Ashura alive through their presence and patience during captivity.
quote: (Condition of the Ahl al-Bayt women:) We are satisfied with God's decree and destiny, and these hardships are easy for us in the path of God.
